module-0-student-enrollment-roster.label: Module 0 Project: Student Enrollment Activity Roster
module-0-student-enrollment-roster.summary: Build a qualified and readable student-enrollment roster through five cumulative join steps.

sketch0.title: Project Overview: Building a Student Enrollment Roster

step3.title: Add Readable Table and Column Aliases

step1.title: Connect Students to Enrollment Records
step1.prompt: Start the module project. In step 1 of 5, Use an INNER JOIN to connect students and enrollments. Return student id, student name, enrollment id, course id, and term.
step1.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

step1.solutionCode:
SELECT 
  students.id, 
  students.name, 
  enrollments.id, 
  enrollments.course_id, 
  enrollments.term 
FROM students 
INNER JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;

step2.title: Qualify Every Shared Identifier
step2.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 2 of 5, Keep the same rows and qualify every selected id column with its table name or alias.
step2.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

SELECT 
  s.id AS student_id, 
  s.name, 
  e.id AS enrollment_id, 
  e.course_id AS course_id, 
  e.term 
FROM students AS s 
INNER JOIN enrollments AS e ON s.id = e.student_id;

step4.title: Focus on Upper Grade Levels
step4.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 4 of 5, Keep the complete joined roster and include only students in grade level 10 or higher.

step4.solutionCode:
SELECT 
  s.id AS student_id, 
  s.name, 
  e.id AS enrollment_id, 
  e.course_id AS course_id, 
  e.term 
FROM students AS s 
INNER JOIN enrollments AS e ON s.id = e.student_id 
WHERE s.grade_level >= 10;

  e.id AS enrollment_id,

  e.term 
FROM students AS s 
INNER JOIN enrollments AS e ON s.id = e.student_id

sketch0.title: Project Overview: Building a Student Enrollment Roster
sketch0.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you will create a comprehensive student enrollment roster by joining data from the `students` and `enrollments` tables. The goal is to produce a readable and qualified report that lists each student's enrollment activities. You'll use SQL joins to connect these tables, ensuring that each enrollment record is accurately linked to the corresponding student. This project will guide you through five steps, each building on the previous one, to achieve a final, polished roster.
